fields.personalityIdealsBonds:
Zodiac: Sea Seraph, Thalia
Age: 27

Loves cheese!

Personality Trait: I talk to animals as if they’re people.
                               I collect strange treasures.

Ideal: Freedom. No tide, king, or captain should decide where someone belongs.

Bond: I protect animals and wild places because they cannot         always protect themselves.

Flaw: My curiosity gets me into trouble.
          I get restless if I stay in one place too long.
          Scared of small and closed of spaces
fields.appearance:
Averag height. 
Long wavy brown/red hair that is often braided with feathers and small trinkets.
Sea green eyes, and sun kissed skin. 
She wears practical pirates clothing. Favoring practical leather and weathered fabrics over anything fancy. A fitted leather corset and belts keep her gear close, while her sleeves from her white blouse are rolled up for freedom of movement. Leather pants with some small rope wrapped around the legs and the Boots are scuffed and strapped, ready for ship decks or rocky shores alike.
Small treasures collected from distant shores like shells, bits of sea glass, and unusual coins hang from cords and pouches on her belt.
A persistent seagull is often found nearby.
At first glance Mara appears to be a carefree pirate chasing adventure, but beneath that free-spirited exterior lies a woman deeply connected to the ocean and fiercely protective of the creatures and wild places she calls home.
She carries herself with confidence and there is something unmistakably wild about her
fields.equipmentInventory:
* Herbalism Kit
* Traveler’s Clothes
* Bedroll
* Waterskin
* Rations (7)
* Rope
* Net
* Cheese
* Sling
* Sling Stones (30)
* Arrows
* Quiver
* Druidic Focus (sprig of mistletoe)

feats.name: Natural Blubber
feats.details: As the Selkie became one with their seal friends, they adapted to the chilling cold of the water by adopting a thin layer of natural blubber below their skin, granting them resistance to cold damage.

feats.name: Friend of Fauna
feats.short: Able to speak to all animals.
feats.details: The Selkie are fey that are naturally friendly towards animals. Although they’ve bonded specifically with seals, they are innately able to speak with all animals.

feats.name: Fey Ancestry
feats.short: Advantage on saving throws against Charmed.
feats.details: Selkies are a type of fey. Therefore, they have Advantage on saving throws that they make to avoid or end the Charmed condition.

feats.name: Natural Adept
feats.short: Advantage on foraging and can sleep with no shelter.
feats.details: Selkies have abandoned the Frontier of Dawn, willingly choosing to survive in the harsh circumstances of the Material Plane in order to live with their chosen companions: the seals. Therefore, Selkies have become adept at survival. They have Advantage on foraging and are used to sleeping outside without shelter.

feats.name: Selkie's Nature
feats.short: Wis or Seal; swim of 40, AC13+Wis, Temp hp 3xlvl
feats.details:
Though it began with donning the pelts of seals that they had befriended, forced evolution caused the Selkies to bond with the pelts and companions on a genetic level. In the present day, Selkies can choose to follow their instincts or fight them with a Wisdom saving throw (DC 10), as they are instinctually driven to transform into a seal when they enter a body of water.

Transforming into a seal, they gain the ability to breathe underwater for up to an hour and gain a swimming speed of 40 ft.

While in the shape of a seal, the Selkie’s Armor Class is equal to 13 + their Wisdom modifier if that total is higher than the Natural Seal’s AC of 15. In addition, Selkies gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to three times their level.

feats.name: Favored Enemy
feats.short: Cast Hunter's Mark without spell slot.
feats.details:
You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared.
You can cast it twice without expending a spell slot, and you regain all expended uses of this ability when you finish a Long Rest.

The number of times you can cast the spell without a spell slot increases when you reach certain Ranger Levels, as shown in the Favored Enemy column of the Ranger Features table.

feats.name: Weapon Mastery
feats.details:
Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Longbows and Shortswords.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For Example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Scimitars and Longswords.

feats.name: Advocate for the Wild
feats.short: Environmental Awareness You’ve learned to recognize imbalance in nature and society.
feats.details:
Environmental Awareness
You’ve learned to recognize imbalance in nature and society. You can spend 10 minutes observing a natural environment, settlement, or social setting to determine whether its natural balance has been disrupted and, if so, in what manner. The DM provides relevant information that aligns with your observations, including social tension, toxic environments, exploitation, environmental strain, pollution, and resource exhaustion.

Deter Violence
As a Reaction, you can deter the attack roll of a creature within 30 feet of you. The attacker must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your chosen modifier) or suffer the following effects:

• The attack that triggered your Reaction must be made with Disadvantage.
• The attacker cannot take a Reaction until the start of its next turn.

This feature can be used a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus per Long Rest. Choose Intelligence or Charisma to determine the DC for this feat.

Source: Mythical Blue

feats.name: Crossbow Expert
feats.short: Ignore loading, Firing in Melee, Dual Wielding
feats.details:
Ignore Loading. You ignore the Loading property of the Hand Crossbow, Heavy Crossbow, and Light Crossbow (all called crossbows elsewhere in this feat). If you're holding one of them, you can load a piece of ammunition into it even if you lack a free hand.

Firing in Melee. Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with crossbows.

Dual Wielding. When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the extra attack if that attack is with a crossbow that has the Light property and you aren't already adding that modifier to the damage.

spells.name: Alarm
spells.effect: You set an alarm against intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range that is no larger than a 20-foot Cube.
spells.details:
You set an alarm against intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range that is no larger than a 20-foot Cube. Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a creature touches or enters the warded area. When you cast the spell, you can designate creatures that won’t set off the alarm. You also choose whether the alarm is audible or mental:

Audible Alarm. The alarm produces the sound of a handbell for 10 seconds within 60 feet of the warded area.

Mental Alarm. You are alerted by a mental ping if you are within 1 mile of the warded area. This ping awakens you if you’re asleep.
spells.componentsText: V, S, M (a bell and silver wire)

spells.name: Ensnaring Strike
spells.castTime: Bonus Action, which you take immedi - ately after hitting a creature with a weapon
spells.effect: As you hit the target, grasping vines appear on it, and it makes a Strength saving throw.
spells.details:
As you hit the target, grasping vines appear on it, and it makes a Strength saving throw. A Large or larger creature has Advantage on this save. On a failed save, the target has the Restrained condition until the spell ends. On a successful save, the vines shrivel away, and the spell ends. While Restrained, the target takes 1d6 Piercing damage at the start of each of its turns. The target or a creature within reach of it can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC. On a success, the spell ends.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Hunter’s Mark
spells.effect: You magically mark one creature you can see within range as your quarry.
spells.details:
You magically mark one creature you can see within range as your quarry. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 Force damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll. You also have Advantage on any Wisdom (Perception or Survival) check you make to find it. If the target drops to 0 Hit Points before this spell ends, you can take a Bonus Action to move the mark to a new creature you can see within range.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 3–4 (up to 8 hours) or 5+ (up to 24 hours).

inventoryItems.name: Arrows
inventoryItems.details:
Ammunition · Weight: 1 lb.

Ammunition from SRD 5.2.1.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Bedroll
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 7 lb.

A Bedroll sleeps one Small or Medium creature. While in a Bedroll, you automatically succeed on saving throws against extreme cold (see “Gameplay Toolbox”).

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Druidic Focus (sprig of mistletoe)
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: Varies

A Druidic Focus takes one of the forms in the Druidic Focuses table and is carved, tied with ribbon, or painted to channel primal magic. A Druid or Ranger can use such an object as a Spellcasting Focus. Focus Weight Cost Sprig of mistletoe — 1 GP Wooden staff (also a Quarterstaff) 4 lb. 5 GP Yew wand 1 lb. 10 GP

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Herbalism Kit
inventoryItems.details:
Tool

Ability: Intelligence Weight: 3 lb. Utilize: Identify a plant (DC 10) Craft: Antitoxin, Candle, Healer’s Kit, Potion of Healing

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Net
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 3 lb.

When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a Net. Target a creature you can see within 15 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or have the Restrained condition until it escapes. The target succeeds automatically if it is Huge or larger. To escape, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 10 Strength (Athletics) check, freeing the Restrained creature on a success. Destroying the Net (AC 10; 5 HP; Immunity to Bludgeoning, Poison, and Psychic damage) also frees the target, ending the effect.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Quiver
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 1 lb.

A Quiver holds up to 20 Arrows.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Rations
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 2 lb.

Rations consist of travelready food, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See “Malnutrition” in “Rules Glossary” for the risks of not eating.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Rope
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 5 lb.

As a Utilize action, you can tie a knot with Rope if you succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. The Rope can be burst with a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check. You can bind an unwilling creature with the Rope only if the creature has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition. If the creature’s legs are bound, the creature has the Restrained condition until it escapes. Escaping the Rope requires the creature to make a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Sling
inventoryItems.details:
Weapon · Weight: —

1d4 Bludgeoning; Ammunition (Range 30/120; Bullet); Slow

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Sling Stones
inventoryItems.details:
Ammunition · Weight: 1½ lb.

Ammunition from SRD 5.2.1.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Traveler’s Clothes
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 4 lb.

Traveler’s Clothes are resilient garments designed for travel in various environments.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Waterskin
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 5 lb. (full)

A Waterskin holds up to 4 pints. If you don’t drink sufficient water, you risk dehydration (see “Rules Glossary”). A mount can help you move more quickly through the wilderness, but its primary purpose is to carry gear that would otherwise slow you down. The Mounts and Other Animals table shows each animal’s carrying capacity. See “Monsters” for the animals’ stat blocks. An animal pulling a carriage, cart, chariot, sled, or wagon can move weight up to five times its base carrying capacity, including the weight of the vehicle. If multiple animals pull the same vehicle, add their carrying capacities together. Barding is armor designed for a mount. Any type of armor on the Armor table in “Equipment” can be purchased as barding. The cost is four times the normal cost, and it weighs twice as much. A saddle comes with a bit, a bridle, reins, and any other equipment needed to use the saddle. A Military Saddle gives Advantage on any ability check you make to remain mounted. An Exotic Saddle is required for riding an aquatic or a flying mount. Mounts and Other Animals Item Carrying Capacity Cost Camel 450 lb. 50 GP Elephant 1,320 lb. 200 GP Horse, Draft 540 lb. 50 GP Horse, Riding 480 lb. 75 GP Mastiff 195 lb. 25 GP Mule 420 lb. 8 GP Pony 225 lb. 30 GP Warhorse 540 lb. 400 GP Item Weight Cost Carriage 600 lb. 100 GP Cart 200 lb. 15 GP Chariot 100 lb. 250 GP Feed per day 10 lb. 5 CP Saddle Exotic 40 lb. 60 GP Military 30 lb. 20 GP Riding 25 lb. 10 GP Sled 300 lb. 20 GP Stabling per day — 5 SP Wagon 400 lb. 35 GP The Airborne and Waterborne Vehicles table provides statistics for various types of large vehicles. The following notes apply. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can’t move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can’t be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

Source: SRD 5.2.1
